mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 8.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, which boosts corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE drops below 8.0 within a 3-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production combined with stable consumer credit balances indicates a synchronized supply-demand recovery that expands profit margins, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising INDPRO and stable RSAFS fails to predict positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rising inflation, the Federal Reserve policy response dictates equity performance; when CPIAUCSL increases by more than 0.2 percent month-over-month, hawkish expectations compress equity multiples and generate negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive in over 50% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 0.2% MoM threshold during tightening phases.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in housing starts reflects improving business and consumer confidence, which translates into higher aggregate demand and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a weekly horizon.
failure_condition: Weekly forward returns show no significant correlation with HOUST growth rates over a 3-year rolling window (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index exceeds its 12-month moving average, manufacturing capacity utilization peaks, signaling strong corporate earnings momentum that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns are negative in more than 55% of instances where INDPRO trades above its 12-month average.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in the unemployment rate and growth in real GDP per employee indicate a productivity-driven labor market expansion that supports corporate profitability and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal of falling UNRATE and rising GDPC1 fails to predict positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: In a credit-constrained environment, rapid growth in consumer credit balances acts as a leading indicator of consumption recovery, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a weekly horizon when credit spreads are tight.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain negative in over 50% of months where RSAFS growth exceeds 1% during credit normalization phases.
